LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF VICTORIA MILLER'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO ADD BATTERY CAUSE OF ACTION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED BATTERY CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE A TOUCHING. SEE CACI 1300; CAL. CASUALTY GEN. INS. CO. V. SUP. CRT. (1983) 173 CAL.APP.3D 274, 280 (DISAPPROVED ON OTHER GROUNDS IN KRANSCO V. AMERICAN EMPIRE SURPLUS LINES INS. CO. (2000) 23 CAL.4TH 390, 407). PLAINTIFF WITHDREW THE MOTION AS TO THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Set For Trial On Jun-01-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Battery Cause Of Action And Punitive Damages Claim
Off Calendar Dec-29-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MARK J. ZANOBINI, Tel No. (415) 392-8400 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. PETER F. FINN of Bradley Curley Asiano Barrabee Abel & Kowalski, APC, Tel No. (415) 464-8888 appearing for the Defendant Paul E. Auger, DDS.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF VICTORIA MILLER's Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Battery Cause Of Action And Punitive Damages Claim is DENIED. Plaintiff's proposed battery cause of action fails as a matter of law because plaintiff does not allege a touching. See CACI 1300; Cal. Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1983) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 280 (disapproved on other grounds in Kransco v. American Empire Surplus Lines Ins. Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 390, 407). Plaintiff withdrew the motion as to the claim for punitive damages. DFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)
